div1.entryFree:
Cling, 1) intr. to adhere closely, to twine round: how they clung in their embracement, H8 I, 1, 9. two spent swimmers, that do c. together and choke their art, Mcb. I, 2, 8.
2) trans. to shrink or shrivel up: upon the next tree shalt thou hang alive, till famine c. thee, Mcb. V, 5, 40.
